data.title: KeyCorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.08, revenue of $695M
data.description:
• KeyCorp press release (NYSE:KEY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $695M (-55.7% Y/Y).

data.title: Capricor Therapeutics stock falls after pricing $75M equity offering
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) fell 12.2% in premarket trade, after its public offering to raise $75M at $17 apiece.
  • The shares are being offering at a discount of 17% as of its last close on Wednesday.
  • Under the underwritten offering, the firm will offer 4.4M shares of common stock.

data.title: Biden to forgive additional $4.5B in student loans
data.description:
The Biden administration said on Thursday that it is forgiving an additional $4.5B in student loans for over 60,000 public service workers through its Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program.

The latest relief brings the number of public service workers who have had their student loans cancelled to over 1M people during the current administration.

“In total, the Biden-Harris Administration has approved $175B in student debt relief for nearly 5 million borrowers through various actions,” the White House said in a briefing.

The PSLF program is a U.S. government program that enables public service workers to have their federal student loans cancelled after a decade in repayment. The government today also announced a series of new steps to encourage public workers to take advantage of the PSLF program.

Tickers related to student loans: SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Navient (NAVI), Nelnet (NNI), SLM Corp. (SLM).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Texas Capital Bancshares Non-GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.65, revenue of $125.33M
data.description:
• Texas Capital Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:TCBI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.65.
  • Revenue of $125.33M (-55.1% Y/Y).
  • Capital ratios continue to be strong, including 11.2% CET1 and 15.2% Total Capital.
  • Provision for credit losses for the third quarter of 2024 was $10.0 million, compared to $20.0 million for the second quarter of 2024. The $10.0 million provision for credit losses recorded in the third quarter of 2024 resulted primarily from growth in total loans held for investment (“LHI”) and $6.1 million in net charge-offs.

• Commerce Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:CBSH): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.07 beats by $0.06.

data.title: Truist Financial beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates
data.description:
• Truist Financial press release (NYSE:TFC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.31 beats by $0.41.
  • Revenue of $5.14B (-10.3% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Net income available to common shareholders was $1.3 billion, or $0.99 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted PPNR was $2.3 billion, up 4.4%.
  • Net interest income increased 2.2%; net interest margin was up 10 basis points ◦ Noninterest income was up 3.1% due to higher investment banking and trading income and other income.
  • Liquidity levels remain strong with consolidated average LCR of 112%.

data.title: Nasdaq futures rise after robust results from TSMC revive confidence in tech stocks
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) helped revive investor sentiments.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.04%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.96%.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 7% in premarket trade, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

On the macroeconomic side, the initial jobless claims and the September retail sales are both expected at the same time. The former is forecasted to fall to 241K, while the latter is seen to rise 0.3% on a monthly basis. Core retail sales is anticipated to come in at 0.1%.

The October Philly Fed business outlook will also land before the bell. The September industrial production and the Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.
